posts.author: ohpleaseuhhelpame
posts.title: Storms bring luck to whatever the occasion. They also make events a little bit more exciting! We will wait it out, I don’t care if it’s 2:00 O’Clock in the morning, or in one hour from now. Looks like it is going to pass, they always do.

link_metadata.description: Storms bring luck to whatever the occasion. They also make events a little bit more exciting! We will wait it out, I don’t care if it’s 2:00 O’Clock in the morning, or in one hour from now. Looks like it is going to pass, they always do. I will be there no matter what, but the “what” usually turns out to be a good thing. It’s Saturday night, LETS HAVE SOME FUN, even if we are out late tonight. They say 11:00 O’Clock for the speech. Who cares??? Remember two weeks ago when the big UFC Event was at 100% chance of heavy rain, all week long, and just minutes before the card started. Well, there wasn’t a single drop, and the fights turned out to be among the greatest in history, an event for the ages. Our great veterans, especially the old timers, many of whom are there, went through hellfire, and it didn’t stop them. It’s not going to stop us either! I’m not going to let some rain stop our 250th. I’m leaving the White House soon. God Bless America! President DONALD J. TRUMP
link_metadata.title: Donald J. Trump (@realDonaldTrump)

posts.raw_content:
Happy 250th Independence Day America! Cheering for you guys from the Philippines!

It's interesting how, as I learn more about history, I realize just how much my country owes to America. Despite more than three centuries of Spanish rule, and even with most of our names (and most likely a good percentage of our genetics) deriving from Spain, I can say for certain that America now has a much greater influence on our country today, even from less than half a century of having been occupied by the US.

My own country's history - or at least the narrative that's regularly taught to us here - is based around a revolutionary spirit against Spain that is more than likely a bizarre copy of the truth behind America's repudiation of the British. It's funny how I learned recently that the Spanish-American War that freed us from Spain was more likely instigated by journalists waging a propaganda war, escalating all the way to the Treaty of Paris that gave us over to the US.

My country regards July 4 as Philippine-American Friendship Day because our actual independence was signed on July 4, 1946 when the US gave the Philippines its freedom to self-govern. To this day, after almost 400 years of being a colony, my country is still struggling to understand what it means to be independent.

Whatever the true story is, I am inspired whenever I look at America and see what it really means to declare independence and fight to defend one's freedom. And it is nothing short of God's divine providence that has kept America free for its 250 years. I pray that America finds its way back to the ideals that freed them from an oppressive crown and let freedom ring all over the world.

Happy 250th Birthday! May God bless the United States of America!
